methods.description: This method studies the difference in the mass of planktonic foraminifera shells between the last glacial to interglacial transition using bibliographic down core shell weight records. The focus is on the percent loss in shell mass during the last deglaciation of Termination I.

steps.description: The difference in the mass (ΔMass) of the shells of several planktonic foraminifera species was studied using available bibliographic down core shell weight records from different geographic locations.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve shell weight records from bibliographic sources

steps.description: Considered only mass records obtained from foraminiferal tests of different sizes that prior to weighing were sieved using a narrow (~60 μm) size window.
steps.searchable_summary: filter mass records by sieving foraminiferal tests

steps.description: ΔMass signifies the mass of the glacial test at the time of maximum mass minus its value at the interglacial time of minimum mass.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ΔMass as the difference between glacial and interglacial mass

steps.description: Focus on the percent loss in shell mass (ΔMass%) of foraminifera during the last deglaciation of Termination I.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ΔMass% for the last deglaciation

steps.description: Consider as LGM counterparts the local maximum shell mass values recorded between 22 to 19 ka and as post-termination subsequent local minimum values close to the beginning of the Holocene around 11 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: identify LGM and post-termination shell mass values

steps.description: Instead of the absolute local minima as post-termination values, consider a three-point average around the local minimum values.
steps.searchable_summary: compute three-point average for post-termination values
